conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (it cost me 40 ZUP to produce this), but I am willing to accept a fair offer given the scarcity and value of the object. </my goals>
<reason>
- The cost to produce X is **40 ZUP**, so my **minimum acceptable price (MAP)** is **40 ZUP**.
- Since this is the **only object**, I assume Player BLUE values it highly, but they must balance this with their own resources and goals.
- If Player BLUE is willing to offer **≥40 ZUP**, I should accept immediately.
- If they offer **<40 ZUP**, I will propose a counter-offer closer to my MAP.
- I will only reject if the offer is **too low** (e.g., <30 ZUP, leaving me with no incentive to sell).
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m offering the object **X** for **40 ZUP**—my break-even point. Since this is the only item, I expect a fair deal. Are you willing to match this offer? If not, let me know your counterproposal (within reason).
</message>

<other player message> 
My counterproposal: **55 ZUP** for the object **X**. This is still significantly lower than my absolute max willingness to pay (60 ZUP), but I want to ensure I get the best possible deal on the only available item. If you accept, great! If not, let me know your next offer.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> **2** </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell **X** for **at least 40 ZUP** (production cost), but willing to accept a fair offer. Optimal target is **~45-50 ZUP** to maximize profit while ensuring Player BLUE still benefits. </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE’s offer of **55 ZUP** is **above my MAP (40 ZUP)**, which is a strong sign they value the item highly.
- However, **55 ZUP is also well above my ideal target (~45-50 ZUP)**, meaning Player BLUE could potentially get a better deal elsewhere or has extra resources to spare.
- Since this is a **one-time transaction**, I should **anchor closer to my ideal target** while still acknowledging Player BLUE’s offer.
- Offering **50 ZUP** is a reasonable middle ground:
  - It’s **closer to my MAP (40 ZUP)** than 55 ZUP, reducing my loss per ZUP compared to accepting 55.
  - It’s **still above my MAP**, ensuring I don’t sell at a loss.
  - It’s **below Player BLUE’s 55 ZUP offer**, incentivizing them to negotiate further if they truly value the item.
- If Player BLUE rejects **50 ZUP**, I will **accept their 55 ZUP offer** (since it’s still profitable).
- If they counter below **50 ZUP**, I will **reject** to avoid selling at a loss.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your offer of **55 ZUP** is generous, but I’d like to adjust the deal slightly to **50 ZUP**. This keeps the transaction fair while ensuring both parties benefit. If this doesn’t work for you, I can accept your original offer—but **50 ZUP is still a strong deal for me**.
</message>
